title: Design a News Feed
interviewPrompt: For today's Software Engineer interview, design a news feed.

beats.interviewer: For today's Software Engineer interview, design a news feed.
beats.interviewee: High level, two ways to build the timeline. Fan-out on write: when you post, copy it into every follower's feed cache, kept in Redis. Or fan-out on read: build the feed when someone opens the app.
beats.show: Two options: fan-out on write vs fan-out on read.

beats.interviewee: Fan-out on write is great for reads, one lookup. But it breaks the moment you have a celebrity, because one post is 50 million writes.
beats.show: Write fan-out = one-lookup reads, but a celebrity is 50M writes.

beats.interviewer: So dig into that celebrity case. What do you do?
beats.interviewee: You do both. Fan-out on write for normal users, and for the handful of mega-accounts, skip the fan-out and merge their posts in at read time.
beats.show: Hybrid: precompute normal users, merge celebrity posts at read time.

beats.interviewee: Gate the fan-out on whether a follower is actually active, so you're not precomputing feeds for accounts that never open the app, which is most of them.
beats.show: Only fan out to active followers; skip dormant accounts.

beats.interviewee: The whole trick is precompute the common path, and compute the expensive path on demand.
beats.show: Precompute the common path; compute the expensive path lazily.
